name: Cape Town
tagline: The Mother City — mountains, beaches, and world-class wine country
description: Cape Town is one of the world's most visited cities, offering Table Mountain, the Cape Peninsula, vibrant neighbourhoods, and the start of the legendary Garden Route.
road_type: Mix of urban roads, mountain passes, and coastal highways

name: Kruger National Park
description: Kruger National Park is one of Africa's largest game reserves. A self-drive safari is the most popular way to experience it.
road_type: Mostly paved internal roads; gravel roads to remote camps
driving_difficulty: Easy to moderate — speed limits strictly enforced
peak_season: June to September (dry season, best game viewing)

name: Garden Route
tagline: South Africa's most iconic road trip
description: The Garden Route stretches from Mossel Bay to Storms River, passing through Knysna, Plettenberg Bay, and the Tsitsikamma forest. It is best explored by car.
road_type: Excellent paved highways (N2) with scenic detours

name: Drakensberg
tagline: Dramatic mountain scenery and ancient San rock art
description: The uKhahlamba-Drakensberg Park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site offering hiking, wildlife, and stunning escarpment views.
road_type: Tarred provincial roads with some gravel access roads to resorts

name: Cape Winelands
tagline: World-class wine, Cape Dutch architecture, and mountain valleys
description: Stellenbosch, Franschhoek, and Paarl form the heart of the Winelands — a 45-minute drive from Cape Town.
road_type: Excellent tarred roads through scenic valleys
